prompt: Students who score above 80 on an entrance exam receive a scholarship. I want to estimate the causal effect of the scholarship on first-year GPA. I have data on 2000 students: exam_score (continuous, 40-100), received_scholarship (binary), first_year_gpa (continuous, 0-4.0), and some covariates (family_income, high_school_gpa). The threshold is 80. Build me a causal analysis.
assertions: Identifies this as a Regression Discontinuity Design
Proposes a DAG or states the causal identification strategy (threshold assignment creates local randomization)
States the key assumption: no manipulation at the threshold (students can't precisely control their score)
Asks the user whether students could manipulate their score to be above/below 80
Uses a bandwidth parameter to restrict analysis to observations near the threshold
Runs at least one robustness check: bandwidth sensitivity, McCrary density test, placebo threshold, or covariate balance
Reports the Local Average Treatment Effect (LATE) at the threshold, not a global ATE
Notes that the effect is local to students near the 80-point threshold, not generalizable to all students
Reports the effect with a credible interval (HDI)
